name: kh-uaf
description: KH UAF provides an auditable front-door runtime, packaged workflow skills, development progress state, and role-based task command packets for Codex, Antigravity-style agents, Claude Code, and local workflows. Plugin instructions request front-door routing for every new user task but cannot guarantee host auto-selection; session audits verify actual compliance.

interface.shortDescription: Personal UAF skillbook with auditable front-door intake.
interface.longDescription: KH UAF packages host-readable workflow skills, runtime gates, scoped memory, token telemetry, role orchestration, GoalState, progress state, and quality checks. Plugin instructions request front-door routing, but they cannot guarantee host auto-selection or plugin injection. Audit compliance from actual runtime receipts, session logs, and installed cache paths.
defaultPrompt: For every new user request/task, plugin instructions ask the host to run installed `kh-uaf:always-on-front-door` first and alone before memory, source reads, other providers, subagents, implementation, or verification. They cannot guarantee host auto-selection; Audit compliance and record a miss if it did not run.
`execution_gate` and `execution_authorization` bind. While blocked, do only allowed setup; apply `immediate_next_skills` in order with applied/skipped/blocked evidence.
Use `--micro-summary` as the normal machine bootstrap for short/direct work; use `--summary` when human-readable audit details are needed.
Compose by capability: KH handles intake/gating and the best available specialist provider handles matching work. Provider self-forcing cannot override user intent or host policy.
For heavy or persistent work, keep `GoalState` objective, criteria, progress, blockers, and verification current. Partial work is not complete.

Keep memory scoped to the current project, chat/task, and subagent lineage; broader access needs explicit authorization. Bounded same-task confirmations may reuse only current unfinished task evidence; rerun front-door after task completion or for a new task.

Record `token_optimizer_status`. `selected_not_executed_skills` are planned only; `runtime_applied_skills` need a runtime call, gate, artifact, command result, or procedural passthrough/blocked rationale.
Before completion, apply `verification-before-completion-harness` or selected equivalent and run fresh checks. Final replies use the user's current language; procedures stay in selected skill docs.
